proof_boundary: This protocol defines how to produce prediction-accuracy evidence. It is not accuracy proof, benchmark proof, hosted proof, or world-class forecasting evidence until owner-approved resolved forecasts and real comparable baselines are scored under the protocol.

protocol_stages.required_artifact: forecast question register with resolution criteria
protocol_stages.pass_condition: Each question has binary or explicitly typed outcome, close time, resolution source, ambiguity handling, and exclusion rule before forecasts are scored.
protocol_stages.claim_boundary: No question without pre-registered resolution criteria can support accuracy claims.

protocol_stages.required_artifact: immutable forecast snapshot or approved export
protocol_stages.pass_condition: Every scored probability has a timestamp strictly before resolution and records source_id, model/policy, prompt or workflow version, and evidence bundle reference.
protocol_stages.claim_boundary: Post-resolution, edited, or timestamp-missing probabilities are excluded.

protocol_stages.pass_condition: At least 25 binary resolved rows exist for one scored source before first pilot accuracy reporting.
protocol_stages.claim_boundary: Below the sample threshold, reports are mechanics proof only.

protocol_stages.required_artifact: calibration ledger with source summaries and reliability bins
protocol_stages.pass_condition: Brier score, sample size, reliability bins, excluded rows, and calibration caveats are reported with 5 planned bins unless owner changes the protocol.
protocol_stages.claim_boundary: Brier/reliability metrics support calibration-aware reporting, not superiority by themselves.

protocol_stages.pass_condition: App forecasts are compared with explicit trivial, human/community/pro, market, or external baselines on same or documented comparable questions.
protocol_stages.claim_boundary: No world-class language without real comparable baselines and source URLs.

protocol_stages.pass_condition: Review confirms forecasts were made before outcomes, retrieval sources were not post-resolution-only, and benchmark questions were not trained/evaluated with known answers.
protocol_stages.claim_boundary: Any unresolved leakage concern downgrades the result to internal diagnostic only.

protocol_stages.pass_condition: Hosted calibration/release jobs and evaluation-table access boundaries are verified before public buyer claims.
protocol_stages.claim_boundary: Local scoring can support internal readiness, not hosted/commercial claims.

protocol_stages.pass_condition: Market copy maps to the achieved evidence tier and keeps uncertainty, sample size, and baseline scope visible.
protocol_stages.claim_boundary: The default allowed phrase remains calibration-aware decision support.

metric_suite.interpretation: Lower is better; report sample size and source_id with every score.
Report observed frequency, count, and calibration error for non-empty bins.

metric_suite.purpose: Shows whether the app beats a specific baseline.
metric_suite.interpretation: Only meaningful when baseline questions and sample scope are comparable.
Report how many eligible questions were scored, excluded, unresolved, ambiguous, or abstained.

metric_suite.purpose: Shows whether forecasts were made early enough to matter.
metric_suite.interpretation: Older standing forecasts and late forecasts should be separated when buyer decisions depend on lead time.

metric_suite.purpose: Connects accuracy to the strategic decision workflow.
metric_suite.interpretation: Track whether a question informed a buyer action, review decision, or forecast registry handoff.

examples: community prediction mean from approved export
baseline_policy.minimum_evidence: same question set, timestamp before resolution, sample size, and role labels
baseline_policy.allowed_claim: compares favorably to internal baseline on this sample

examples: Good Judgment/custom forecaster baseline where available
baseline_policy.minimum_evidence: source URL, same or explicitly comparable question mapping, sample size, and scoring method
baseline_policy.allowed_claim: compared with an external forecasting baseline on a documented question set

baseline_policy.minimum_evidence: de-vig or conversion method when applicable, timestamp, source URL, and benchmark protocol notes
baseline_policy.allowed_claim: benchmark-aware comparison; no broad superiority unless independently reviewed

minimum_conditions: sample fixture or incomplete approved export
claim_tiers.allowed_language: calibration and benchmark mechanics are implemented
claim_tiers.prohibited_language: accurate predictions, best-in-class forecasting, world-class forecasting

minimum_conditions: owner-approved export has at least 25 binary resolved rows for one scored source
Brier score and reliability bins reported
claim_tiers.allowed_language: internal pilot calibration report on an approved sample

minimum_conditions: sample size and caveats visible in buyer packet
claim_tiers.allowed_language: calibration-aware forecasting workflow with measured performance on a documented sample

minimum_conditions: multi-domain resolved sample is large enough for stable buyer review
human/pro/community or benchmark baselines are independently comparable
hosted run, RLS/evaluation-table boundary, and adversarial review pass
claim language is reviewed against current benchmark context
claim_tiers.prohibited_language: world-class accurate predictions without independent review and repeatability

leakage_controls: Forecast timestamp must be before resolution timestamp.
Resolution source URL or notes must be attached for every included row.
Post-resolution retrieval, rationale, or analyst notes cannot be used to generate the scored probability.
Synthetic/sample fixtures must remain tagged source-mode sample_fixture.
Question edits after forecast close must be versioned or excluded.
Comparable baselines must use the same questions or a documented mapping.
Any confidential buyer, user, credential, or direct personal contact/payment data must be redacted before artifacts are committed.
owner_inputs_required_next: approved resolved forecast export with binary outcomes and resolution sources
pre-resolution forecast snapshot or immutable probability records
real comparable baseline file or source URL
hosted calibration/release smoke evidence after approved deploy/secrets
current_blockers: Current ledger status is not_launch_proof with source mode sample_fixture.
Current benchmark status is sample_only_not_launch_proof with 15 sample comparisons.
Current accuracy confidence score is 35 and remains blocked by owner-approved data and baselines.
No protocol stage has been executed against real owner-approved resolved forecasts in this artifact.

external_methodology_alignment.protocol_implication: Use real future-outcome questions and compare with community/pro/human baselines where both humans and bots made forecasts.
Prefer dynamic, contamination-resistant questions whose answers were unknown at forecast time, with human comparison groups where available.
Track difficulty-adjusted Brier or comparable Brier-style scoring and ranking stability rather than one-off anecdotes.
Use proper scoring rules so incentives reward sincere probabilities rather than overconfident predictions.
Document measurable performance changes, field data, monitoring, and risk-response decisions before relying on AI-system claims.
